column1: man reddit stock is just sad to watch. ad revenue is completely stalling and the user growth is fake bot accounts. puts are printing but i feel gross
The pipeline for $PFE is a barren wasteland. They burned all their pandemic cash on terrible acquisitions and now the dividend looks at risk. Total yield trap.
the entire semiconductor sector is flashing massive red warning signs. channel inventory is building up faster than 2018. we are going to crash violently.
I WILL NEVER FORGIVE ROBINHOOD FOR TURNING OFF THE BUY BUTTON. COMPANY DESERVES TO BURN TO THE GROUND. DELETE THE APP.
Absolutely brutal downgrade on $CRWD today. The reputational damage from the global outage is permanent. Enterprise clients are migrating to SentinelOne en masse.
Boeing is a national embarrassment. The sheer amount of technical debt and union strikes makes this a zero in the long run if the government doesn't bail them out.
my weed stocks are literally worth pennies now. i believed the rescheduling hype and got completely rugged. generationally horrible trade.
$SNAP is the cockroach of social media. It just slowly decays, dilutes shareholders, and refuses to die. Who is even buying these shares?
Not touching $SBUX until the new CEO actually proves the turnaround is working. China sales are still incredibly weak and the US traffic is declining.
Ford's warranty costs are absolutely eating them alive. The EV transition is a massive money pit and the legacy ICE business can't subsidize it forever.
cautious on this cloud infrastructure play heading into earnings. the valuation implies 30% growth but IT budgets are clearly tightening globally.
Looking at the fundamental decay of $NKE is fascinating. They completely lost the running shoe market to Hoka and On. Gross margins are getting crushed by discounting.
Delta Airlines guidance cut is a massive canary in the coal mine for the US consumer. Revenge travel is officially dead and corporate bookings are soft.
that AI wrapper startup is going to zero. OpenAI literally just released a native feature that does their entire business model for free. RIP.
im so tired of holding $PYPL. active accounts dropping again. the new management talks a big game but the numbers just keep getting worse.
Pfizer is a classic yield trap right now. Everyone buying it for the 6% dividend is going to lose 15% on the principal. Avoid.
sold my speculative bag today at a 60% loss. sometimes you just have to admit your thesis was wrong and move on. hurts but it is what it is.
The mortgage originations data completely ruins the bull case for $Z. Housing market is completely frozen. I am staying far away.
Honestly just dripping my $KO dividends. It doesn't move fast, but it's basically a high-yield savings account that beats inflation.
Home Depot earnings were exactly what we expected. Flat comps, guidance reaffirmed. A true snooze fest, which is fine by me.
just dcaing into the broad market ETF. stock picking in this macro environment feels like throwing darts blindfolded.
$BRK.B is hoarding record levels of cash. Warren knows something we don't. Just gonna hold my shares and wait for the inevitable correction.
Visa volume metrics indicate consumer spending is holding steady, slightly shifting from discretionary to staples. A very balanced quarter.

Selling puts on $VIX while it's pinned at 12 feels like picking up pennies in front of a steamroller, but the premium is just so juicy.
McDonald's line was packed today during lunch. Value menu promotions seem to be working. Not buying the stock but they aren't dying either.
Delta hedging my options against the underlying index today. Market is trading in a super tight range, pure chop.
Still holding my $T shares from a decade ago. The capital depreciation sucks but at least the debt load is finally starting to tick down.

Spotify price hikes went through with literally zero churn. Pricing power is absolute. Unbelievable moat they have built in audio.
Adding heavily to this cybersecurity ETF. The geopolitical tensions basically guarantee that government cyber spending is going to double over the next 5 years.
